--- title: "Welcome to Interactive Math" subtitle: "Master math concepts at your own pace with endless practice problems." page-layout: full filters: - gradio --- ## Getting Started Test $f(x) = \frac{1}{x}$ Test 2 $g(x)= 3f(x)$ Welcome! This website is designed to give you complete control over your learning. Every lesson features dynamic problems that change numbers every time you refresh or click a button, giving you infinite opportunities to practice. ::: {.callout-tip} ### How to use this site 1. Select a lesson from the top navigation bar. 2. Read the concept overview. 3. Use the interactive sliders to see how math graphs change in real-time. 4. Try the practice problems and type your answers into the check-boxes to test your understanding. ::: --- ## Quick Math Warm-Up Test your mental math before diving into the lessons. Change the multiplier below to see how the sequence changes! ```{ojs} //| echo: false viewof factor = html`` ``` $f(x) = \frac34$ ::: {#a9cbb7b1 .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} x = 5 y = 6 print(x+y) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` 11 ``` ::: ::: $hello$ ::: {#995ee376 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} import gradio as gr def greet(name): return f"Hello {name}!" demo = gr.Interface( fn=greet, inputs="textbox", outputs="textbox", live=True ) demo.launch() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` * Running on local URL: http://127.0.0.1:7862 * To create a public link, set `share=True` in `launch()`. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ```{=html}
